channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Systemic Failures in Canada’s Drinking Water Supply for First Nations: Health and Governance Challenges
item.description: In January 2026, around 200 people evacuated from Kashechewan First Nation arrived in Kingston, Ontario, many with complex medical needs. Indigenous healthcare teams quickly set up a mobile clinic to ...

item.title: South African Government Engages UFS on Safety of Menstrual Hygiene Products
item.description: The South African Department of Women, Youth and Persons with Disabilities (DWYPD) has engaged the University of the Free State (UFS) following a peer-reviewed study that found endocrine-disrupting ch...

item.title: Manchester City Edge Past Leeds United at Elland Road
item.description: Manchester City waka go Elland Road dey face Leeds United without their main striker, Erling Haaland, wey dem injury during training. E no easy at all for dem as Leeds start strong, dey put City under...

item.title: Iyabo Obasanjo Joins APC, Declares Intent to Contest Ogun Governorship
item.description: Iyabo Obasanjo, pikin of former President Olusegun Obasanjo, don officially join the All Progressives Congress (APC) as she declare say she wan run for Ogun State governor. She talk say her decision d...

item.title: DepEd approves over 5,000 pending teacher promotions as part of Expanded Career Progression rollout
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has approved 5,335 long-pending teacher promotion requests that were filed under previous promotion guidelines, marking a significant milestone in its transition to...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Condoles Iran Over Khamenei's Death, Condemns Targeting of Leaders
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed deep sorrow over the martyrdom of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei following joint airstrikes by the United States and Israel that started on Februa...

item.title: Jim Carrey Addresses Speculation Over His Appearance at the César Awards
item.description: Jim Carrey, the 64-year-old Canadian-American actor, has addressed recent online speculation regarding his appearance at the 51st César Awards in Paris, which led to outlandish theories suggesting he ...

item.title: House Committee Approves Bill Limiting Political Dynasties in Same Areas
item.description: The House Committee on Suffrage and Electoral Reforms approved House Bill 6771, an anti-political dynasty measure, on March 3, 2026. The bill seeks to prevent close relatives—up to the fourth civil de...

item.title: CAC Registrar Apologises to Senate for Missing Previous Summons
item.description: The Registrar-General of the Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC), Hussaini Magaji, don apologise to the Senate Committee on Finance for him no show for past invitations. This na follow after some senat...

item.title: Tilray Acquires BrewDog, Resulting in 484 Job Losses and Bar Closures
item.description: Nearly 500 jobs are expected to be lost following the acquisition of Scottish craft brewer BrewDog by American cannabis and craft beer company Tilray Brands. BrewDog entered administration after finan...

item.title: Homa Bay opens women-led sanitary pad factory to reduce school absenteeism and create jobs
item.description: Homa Bay County has officially launched the Galentine Care Sanitary Pad Factory in Kasgunga Ward, Suba North, in a bold move to address period poverty, reduce teenage pregnancies and create employment...

item.title: U.S. Cuts Humanitarian Aid Completely in Seven African Countries
item.description: Di Trump administration don decide to stop humanitarian aid wey dem dey give seven African countries, namely Burkina Faso, Cameroon, Malawi, Mali, Niger, Somalia, and Zimbabwe. According to internal e...

item.title: Tottenham Extend Winless Streak with Fulham Loss
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur continue dey waka through rough patch as dem don fail win for 10 Premier League games. Dem last match na against Fulham wey dem lose 2-0. Fulham score early, with Harry Wilson hammer...

item.title: Floods and malaria in Southern Africa: Preparing communities for post-disaster health risks
item.description: When heavy floods hit southern Africa, like in early 2026, the immediate destruction is clear: homes washed away, crops destroyed, clinics disrupted, and families displaced. But after waters go down, ...

item.title: Highlights from the Actor Awards Red Carpet in Los Angeles
item.description: The Actor Awards, formerly known as the SAG Awards, held its first edition under the new name on Sunday night in Los Angeles, attracting numerous stars to celebrate the best screen performances in tel...

item.title: Comelec to Conduct Special Voter Registration for Women at Intramuros HQ on March 9
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Commission on Elections (Comelec) will hold a Special Register Anywhere Program (SRAP) exclusively for women on March 9 at its main office in Intramuros, Manila, as part of t...

item.title: Middle-aged workers and the political challenge in Britain
item.description: Millions of Britons aged between 50 and 64 face barriers to employment, whether due to chronic illness, care responsibilities, or age-related discrimination. Many are sidelined despite possessing valu...

item.title: Impact of Middle East Conflict on Australian Petrol Prices and Cost of Living
item.description: The ongoing conflict involving Iran has begun to ripple through global markets, impacting Australians' cost of living. Since the war erupted, global oil prices have surged more than 8 per cent, with B...

item.title: Saskatchewan benefits from new Canada-India uranium trade agreement
item.description: Saskatchewan Premier Scott Moe has welcomed a new $2.6-billion uranium trade agreement between Canada and India, describing it as a positive development for both the provincial and national economy. U...

item.title: Legal Requirements for Claiming Maintenance from Siblings in South Africa
item.description: In South Africa, individuals who are struggling financially may be entitled to claim maintenance from their siblings under certain legal conditions. This provision, which has existed since 1938 in the...
